description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with visible grain and natural imperfections, suggesting it is crafted from a solid piece of wood. The axe head is made of metal, showing a weathered, darkened surface with patches of discoloration and what appears to be dried red stains, possibly simulating rust or use. The blade is broad and wedge-shaped, with a single sharp edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for chopping or splitting. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with the mounting area showing a tight fit and no visible fasteners. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with signs of wear and use, making it suitable for urban environments such as garages, workshops, or maintenance areas. The object exudes a rugged, practical aesthetic, with no visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, stained metal blade, showing signs of heavy use and wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, stained metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow edge of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the other face of the blade and the handle's curvature.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat poll of the axe head and the back of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, oval cross-section, no added grip features, ergonomic for hand use.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll, no beard, and a broad cutting edge; stained and weathered surface.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted tightly onto the handle.
